description_long: This is a modern, sleek military jet aircraft with a sharply pointed nose, a streamlined fuselage, and a dark, matte finish. The wings are swept back aggressively and are equipped with multiple hardpoints carrying missiles or external fuel tanks. The twin vertical stabilizers at the rear are canted outward, giving the tail a distinctive angular look. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage and features a clear, bubble canopy, likely accommodating a single pilot. The landing gear is visible and appears to be of the tricycle, retractable type, with two main gear units and a nose wheel. The overall design emphasizes speed, maneuverability, and advanced aerodynamics, typical of a modern fighter jet.
description: A dark, modern fighter jet with swept wings, twin outward-canted vertical stabilizers, and a forward bubble canopy cockpit. It is armed with multiple missiles and features retractable tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the sharply pointed nose, forward cockpit, swept wings with mounted missiles, and twin outward-canted vertical stabilizers.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, missile hardpoints, and the angular twin tail configuration.
description_view_2: Opposite side view emphasizing the undercarriage, missile mounts, and the streamlined fuselage profile.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the bubble canopy cockpit, nose cone, and the symmetrical arrangement of missiles and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with multiple hardpoints for external stores; no visible winglets; moderate anhedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: twin jet engines integrated into the rear fuselage
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers canted outward in an H-tail configuration with conventional horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: tricycle, retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose wheel
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed bubble canopy cockpit, likely single-seat
